prompt: A dramatic vertical Chinese ink wash painting in monochrome black, charcoal, and soft gray on off-white rice paper, in the style of expressive shan shui brush art and heroic historical wuxia illustration. On the right side, a lone armored general sits upright on a dark warhorse atop a rocky cliff, viewed from behind and slightly in profile, looking out over a vast army below. The warrior wears layered black lamellar armor, a flowing windblown cloak, and a topknot with long trailing ribbons; he holds a straight sword angled downward beside the horse. The horse is muscular and glossy black, standing firmly near the cliff edge with its tail and mane subtly swept by the wind. Below and across the lower half of the scene, a dense battlefield mass of soldiers appears as misty silhouettes with upright spears and several battle flags, partially obscured by fog. In the distance, steep mountain peaks emerge through drifting mist on the upper right, with 7 small birds flying across the pale sky near the center. On the left side, include 2 large vertical columns of bold black Chinese calligraphy in wild cursive brush strokes: the main quote reading “一身转战三千里，一剑曾当百万师”, with a smaller vertical signature “王维” below, plus a small red seal stamp. Emphasize strong negative space, textured ink bleeding, splattered brush edges, atmospheric haze, epic solitude, martial grandeur, and a solemn, contemplative mood.
